reasoning: The condition for this market requires a sequence of Cut in Dec 2025, Pause in Jan 2026, and Pause in Mar 2026. Based on FOMC statements, the Fed officially cut rates by 25 basis points at the December 10, 2025 meeting, bringing the target range downwards. Subsequently, at the January 28, 2026 meeting, the Fed voted to maintain the rate, delivering the required Pause. Thus, the first two legs of the condition have already been met. For the upcoming March 17-18, 2026 meeting, the most recent CME FedWatch tool data as of late February indicates a 96% probability of another rate hold (Pause), with only a 4% chance of a rate cut. The Polymarket price on February 19 was 0.93, reflecting this strong likelihood. As incoming data continues to strengthen the case for a hold in March, the probability approaches the upper bounds of certainty.
